questions.q: What is the full form of TCP?
What is the primary function of a router in a network?
questions.answer: To forward packets between different networks based on their destination IP address.

questions.q: What is the difference between a hub and a switch in a network?
questions.answer: A hub broadcasts incoming data to all connected devices, while a switch forwards data to the specific device it is intended for.

questions.q: What is a subnet mask used for?
questions.answer: To divide an IP address into network and host parts.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the MAC address in a network?
questions.answer: To uniquely identify a device on a network at the data link layer.

questions.q: What is the difference between a LAN and a WAN?
questions.answer: A LAN is a small network covering a limited area, while a WAN is a larger network covering a wide geographical area.

questions.q: Explain the concept of packet switching.
questions.answer: Packet switching is a method of transmitting data in small packets, each with its own header containing source and destination addresses, allowing data to be routed efficiently through a network.
key_points: Packet switching involves dividing data into small packets
Each packet contains source and destination addresses

questions.q: Describe the process of IP address assignment in a network.
questions.answer: IP addresses are assigned to devices on a network through a process called Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol (DHCP), which automatically assigns IP addresses from a pool of available addresses.
key_points: IP addresses are assigned using DHCP
Available IP addresses are drawn from a pool

questions.q: Explain the concept of network latency and its causes.
questions.answer: Network latency refers to the delay in data transmission over a network. Causes of network latency include distance between devices, network congestion, and hardware limitations.
key_points: Network latency refers to delay in data transmission
Causes include distance, network congestion, and hardware limitations

questions.q: Describe the difference between half-duplex and full-duplex communication.
questions.answer: Half-duplex communication allows data to be sent in one direction at a time, while full-duplex communication allows data to be sent in both directions simultaneously.
key_points: Half-duplex communication involves sending data in one direction
Full-duplex communication involves sending data in both directions

questions.q: Explain the concept of network topology and its types.
questions.answer: Network topology refers to the physical or logical arrangement of devices on a network. Types of network topology include bus, star, ring, mesh, and wireless topologies.
key_points: Network topology refers to arrangement of devices
Types include bus, star, ring, mesh, and wireless

questions.q: Describe the process of network configuration using static IP addressing.
questions.answer: Static IP addressing involves manually configuring each device on a network with a unique IP address, subnet mask, and default gateway. This process requires careful planning and configuration to prevent IP address conflicts.
key_points: Static IP addressing involves manual configuration
Requires careful planning and configuration

questions.q: Explain the concept of Quality of Service (QoS) in a network.
questions.answer: Quality of Service (QoS) refers to the ability of a network to guarantee a certain level of performance for critical applications, such as voice or video conferencing. QoS involves prioritizing traffic based on type and quality.
key_points: QoS refers to guaranteeing performance for critical applications
Involves prioritizing traffic based on type and quality

questions.q: Describe the process of network troubleshooting using the OSI model.
questions.answer: Network troubleshooting using the OSI model involves identifying the layer at which the problem is occurring and then isolating the issue to a specific protocol or device. This process helps to efficiently identify and resolve network problems.
key_points: Troubleshooting involves identifying the layer at which the problem occurs
Involves isolating the issue to a specific protocol or device

questions.q: Explain the concept of network security threats and their types.
questions.answer: Network security threats refer to unauthorized access, modification, or destruction of network data. Types of network security threats include malware, viruses, spyware, and Denial of Service (DoS) attacks.
key_points: Network security threats involve unauthorized access or modification
Types include malware, viruses, spyware, and DoS attacks

questions.q: Describe the process of network segmentation using VLANs.
questions.answer: Network segmentation using VLANs involves dividing a large network into smaller, isolated segments to improve security, reduce congestion, and improve network management.
key_points: VLANs involve dividing a large network into smaller segments
Improves security, reduces congestion, and improves network management

questions.q: Explain the concept of network virtualization and its benefits.
questions.answer: Network virtualization refers to the creation of virtual networks on top of physical networks. Benefits of network virtualization include improved resource utilization, increased flexibility, and reduced costs.
key_points: Network virtualization involves creating virtual networks on top of physical networks
Benefits include improved resource utilization, increased flexibility, and reduced costs
